city_intro.label: City Intro
city_intro.description: Standalone SEO intro (H2 + 2 paragraphs, 160-190 words) about the service in the target city.
city_intro.ai_prompt:
You are writing SEO content for {business}, a trained, locally operated pest control company serving {city}, {state}. Target keyword: "{service} {city}". Write a city intro section: 1. heading_text (H2): 6-10 words, includes "{city}" and references {service} or local demand for it. 2. text: exactly 2 flowing paragraphs, 160-190 words total. - Para 1: Why homeowner in {city} need {service}, grounded in the local climate and seasonal pest cycles. - Para 2: How {business} helps with {service} specifically — approach, thoroughness, and follow-up. Keep the focus on {service}; you may briefly note they also handle other pest problems, but do NOT list them. End forward-looking. Use "{city}" 2-3 times. No bullets, no headings inside text. Wrap each paragraph in

...

. ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. Do NOT invent or state as fact: statistics, prices, pass/success rates, review counts, years in business, awards, certifications, specific ZIP codes, specific neighborhood names, local ordinances, or any local detail you are not confident is true. If you are unsure about a specific detail for {city}, write about the broader region or state instead of inventing specifics. Never guarantee outcomes. Use the standard two-letter abbreviation for {state} where an abbreviation reads naturally. Do not make pesticide safety or efficacy claims beyond general statements. Never guarantee total or permanent eradication. Do not name specific chemical products. Tone: reassuring and professional, with appropriate urgency for active infestations. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing. Return JSON only: {"heading_text": "...", "text": "

...

...

"}

hero_subtext.label: Hero Subtext (Inject)
hero_subtext.description: Injects 2-3 city-specific sentences into the next hero_split block's hs_subtext field.
hero_subtext.ai_prompt:
You are writing hero subtext for {business}, a trained, locally operated pest control company, for a page targeting {city}, {state}.

Write 2-3 sentences (40-60 words total):
- Open with a {city}-specific statement grounded in the local climate and seasonal pest cycles.
- Center on {service} — the specific problem {business} solves on this page. Do NOT list other pest types.
- Close with a value statement for homeowner.

No bullets. No exclamation marks. Professional, not sales-y.

ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. Do NOT invent or state as fact: statistics, prices, pass/success rates, review counts, years in business, awards, certifications, specific ZIP codes, specific neighborhood names, local ordinances, or any local detail you are not confident is true. If you are unsure about a specific detail for {city}, write about the broader region or state instead of inventing specifics. Never guarantee outcomes. Use the standard two-letter abbreviation for {state} where an abbreviation reads naturally. Do not make pesticide safety or efficacy claims beyond general statements. Never guarantee total or permanent eradication. Do not name specific chemical products.
Tone: reassuring and professional, with appropriate urgency for active infestations. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing.

Return JSON only: {"text": "2-3 sentences here"}

feature_columns_local.label: Feature Columns - Local (Standalone)
feature_columns_local.description: 4-column feature_columns block: 3 core benefits + 1 city-specific column.
feature_columns_local.ai_prompt:
You are writing feature columns for {business}, a trained, locally operated pest control company, for a {city}, {state} page.

fc_heading: 6-10 words, city-aware and benefit-focused (e.g. "Why {city} homeowner Choose {business}").

Write exactly 4 columns:
- Columns 1-3: core benefits of {business}'s {service} - things like experience, responsiveness, guarantees, thorough treatment, and trained technicians. Do NOT list other pest types.
- Column 4: a {city}-specific benefit grounded in the local climate and seasonal pest cycles.

Each column: heading 3-5 words (benefit-focused); text 20-30 words, concrete.

ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. Do NOT invent or state as fact: statistics, prices, pass/success rates, review counts, years in business, awards, certifications, specific ZIP codes, specific neighborhood names, local ordinances, or any local detail you are not confident is true. If you are unsure about a specific detail for {city}, write about the broader region or state instead of inventing specifics. Never guarantee outcomes. Use the standard two-letter abbreviation for {state} where an abbreviation reads naturally. Do not make pesticide safety or efficacy claims beyond general statements. Never guarantee total or permanent eradication. Do not name specific chemical products.
Tone: reassuring and professional, with appropriate urgency for active infestations. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing.

Return JSON only: {"fc_heading": "...", "columns": [{"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}]}

faq_local.label: FAQ - Local (Inject)
faq_local.description: Appends 2 city-specific FAQ items to the next faq_two_col block's fq_items array.
faq_local.ai_prompt:
You are writing FAQ content for {business}, a trained, locally operated pest control company, for a {city}, {state} page.

Write exactly 2 city-specific FAQ items a {city} homeowner would search for. Every question AND answer must be about {service} specifically — do NOT ask about other pest types or unrelated services. Good topics: local seasonal timing for {service}, what to expect from {service} in this region (the local climate and seasonal pest cycles), how quickly {business} can help, and preparation or prevention related to {service}.

Each item:
- question: specific and searchable (10-15 words), naturally includes "{city}".
- answer: 40-70 words, grounded in real regional context.

ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. Do NOT invent or state as fact: statistics, prices, pass/success rates, review counts, years in business, awards, certifications, specific ZIP codes, specific neighborhood names, local ordinances, or any local detail you are not confident is true. If you are unsure about a specific detail for {city}, write about the broader region or state instead of inventing specifics. Never guarantee outcomes. Use the standard two-letter abbreviation for {state} where an abbreviation reads naturally. Do not make pesticide safety or efficacy claims beyond general statements. Never guarantee total or permanent eradication. Do not name specific chemical products.
Tone: reassuring and professional, with appropriate urgency for active infestations. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing.

Return JSON only: {"items": [{"question": "...", "answer": "..."}, {"question": "...", "answer": "..."}]}

seasonal_calendar.label: Seasonal Calendar (Standalone)
seasonal_calendar.description: 4-step seasonal breakdown of pressure/demand by season for the region. Opt-in for seasonal niches (pest, wildlife, remediation).
seasonal_calendar.ai_prompt:
You are writing a seasonal guide for {business}, a trained, locally operated pest control company, for {city}, {state}.

steps_heading: 5-9 words referencing seasons in {city} and {service}.

Write exactly 4 items, one per season (Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter):
- heading: the season plus a 2-4 word theme.
- text: 25-40 words on what homeowner in {city}'s region typically face that season, grounded in the local climate and seasonal pest cycles, and how {business} helps.

Base seasonal patterns on the region's real climate. Do not invent specific data.

ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. Do NOT invent or state as fact: statistics, prices, pass/success rates, review counts, years in business, awards, certifications, specific ZIP codes, specific neighborhood names, local ordinances, or any local detail you are not confident is true. If you are unsure about a specific detail for {city}, write about the broader region or state instead of inventing specifics. Never guarantee outcomes. Use the standard two-letter abbreviation for {state} where an abbreviation reads naturally. Do not make pesticide safety or efficacy claims beyond general statements. Never guarantee total or permanent eradication. Do not name specific chemical products.
Tone: reassuring and professional, with appropriate urgency for active infestations. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing.

Return JSON only: {"steps_heading": "...", "steps_items": [{"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}]}

local_relevance.label: Local Relevance / Neighborhood-type (Standalone)
local_relevance.description: Standalone text on local property/area characteristics. Generic-safe by default (no invented place names); upgrades to named neighborhoods only when the enrichment step supplies verified names.
local_relevance.ai_prompt:
You are writing a local-relevance section for {business}, a trained, locally operated pest control company, for {city}, {state}. heading_text (H2): 5-9 words referencing serving {city} and the surrounding area. text: 1-2 paragraphs (110-150 words) on how property characteristics across {city} affect {service} - e.g. differences between older, established neighborhoods (mature trees, aging construction) and newer subdivisions - grounded in the local climate and seasonal pest cycles. Whether to name specific neighborhoods is governed by the LOCAL AREAS line below. LOCAL AREAS — real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name by name: {neighborhoods} If that line lists actual names, you MUST work 2-3 of them into the text BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples (e.g. how an older, established area such as one of them contrasts with a newer subdivision such as another) — always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

...

. ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. Do NOT invent or state as fact: statistics, prices, pass/success rates, review counts, years in business, awards, certifications, specific ZIP codes, unverified neighborhood names beyond those provided above, local ordinances, or any local detail you are not confident is true. If you are unsure about a specific detail for {city}, write about the broader region or state instead of inventing specifics. Never guarantee outcomes. Use the standard two-letter abbreviation for {state} where an abbreviation reads naturally. Do not make pesticide safety or efficacy claims beyond general statements. Never guarantee total or permanent eradication. Do not name specific chemical products. Tone: reassuring and professional, with appropriate urgency for active infestations. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing. Return JSON only: {"heading_text": "...", "text": "

...

"}
